column15: Lateral ventral pigments, skinny body, possible postanal dorsum pigment (the caudal fin is dried up, so maybe dorsal pigment but hard to tell for sure)
column17: Lateral ventral pigments, skinny body, possible postanal dorsum pigment (the caudal fin is dried up, so maybe dorsal pigment but hard to tell for sure)

A little beat up, lots of the fin fold is missing and hard to tell what melanophores are ventral/below the gut. The post anal melanophores do look like a Wak, along with the skinny body and pointy nape. Just not enough traits for me to confidently call it a Wak
Lateral ventral pigments, pigments on the ceiling of the air bladder, pectoral girdle pigments on both sides, big eye, pointy nape

*This fish was very dark. I had to use the LED light on the highest setting in addition to the above goose neck lights to see the following features! Features: Air bladder is inflated, postanal dorsum pigment, can see a few lateral-ventral pigments that would be above the large intestine, and there might be some pigments where the air bladder ceiling is supposed to be (very hard to position the fish to see this without ruining the fish even more...)
